verdict.answer_first: B states it immediately: “Idempotency means that making the same request multiple times produces the same result as making it once”; A also answers early but starts with a title before the definition.
verdict.decisiveness: A is slightly more prescriptive: “Design GET/PUT/DELETE to honor their natural idempotent semantics, and protect POST-style... operations with idempotency keys”; B explains options more generally with “a natural key, an upsert... or... idempotency key.”
verdict.actionability: B is easier to act on right now because it gives the definition, HTTP examples, retry failure mode, and idempotency-key pattern without the extra table and implementation detail.
